pr.title: [codex] Track app-server, thread, and turn timing in ChatGPT telemetry
pr.body:
## Why
We want ChatGPT-facing telemetry for app-server startup, thread start, and per-turn timing breakdowns while keeping turn-only timing data attached to the turn event. This change does that by reusing the existing analytics client and reducer pipeline instead of introducing a separate reporting path.

## What
- add a `codex_app_server_started` event with runtime, RPC transport, completion time, and startup duration
- capture thread start duration in app-server and attach it to `codex_thread_initialized` for new threads
- extend turn timing tracking to record request start delay, cumulative sampling time, and blocking tool critical-path time before emitting the turn event
- roll approval wait time up from review events and expose the remaining `finalize_duration_ms` on `codex_turn`
- add analytics reducer/client coverage and turn timing unit coverage for the new event shapes and timing merge behavior

## Testing
- `cargo check -p codex-analytics -p codex-app-server -p codex-core`
- Not run locally per repo guidance; CI should validate the test suite.
pr.author: aibrahim-oai
